behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request-builder logic at one object/function boundary. A fake client assertion catches omission of labels without real network/OS/model output. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on request content ('includes the complete list'), not cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Call classifier with a fake API client; assert outbound payload contains every configured taxonomy label (order-agnostic if spec allows) on each invocation.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic post-processing/validation logic. Stubbed API response with an out-of-taxonomy label is sufficient; real infra or model behavior is not required. Rejected Integration because failure is in local mapping rule ('returns unknown').
behaviors.test_description: Inject raw provider response containing an invalid label and assert returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to normalization determinism on identical inputs, which is pure local logic. Rejected higher levels because text says 'identical raw API responses' and tests only normalization output stability.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization repeatedly on the same raw response fixture and assert byte-for-byte same final label each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real model/provider behavior under repeated live calls; mocks would hide nondeterminism and real drift. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-agent/multi-decision pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Against the real provider, issue N repeated calls for fixed document+taxonomy, record provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label changes only when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is local pre-dispatch budget policy (truncate/reject) in request construction. Can be validated with deterministic token-count fixtures and a fake dispatch sink. Rejected Integration because behavior text is explicitly 'before dispatch'.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed budget and inputs around boundary sizes, assert builder truncates or rejects per policy and never calls dispatch when policy requires rejection.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Must observe real provider-reported usage from real API/model execution; stubs can mask tokenizer/provider mismatches. Although assertion is deterministic, behavior executes through real AI API and depends on provider token accounting. Rejected Workflow because it is a single-call budget check, not multi-step agent chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative documents to the real provider, capture provider usage metadata, and assert reported total tokens per call <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundary between API client signaling rate-limit and retry/backoff orchestration. In-process fake client + controllable clock can reproduce this without real network/OS. Integration brake checks: no required real OS/network behavior, no dependence on model output quality, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier + retry policy + fake API adapter: first response is rate-limit, next is success; assert wait/backoff occurs and caller receives successful label instead of immediate error.
